9037.text: Where a party seeks an order that the Court fee associated with his claim should be waived:

9038.text: (1) He must state this in his claim form;

9039.text: (2) He must set out in his claim form or attach to his claim form a statement of the grounds on which he seeks waiver of the court fees;

9040.text: (3) The SCT Registrar shall decide on paper whether to waive the fees or defer payment of the fees until the end of the case;

9041.text: (4) Where Court fees are waived, the SCT Registry shall record that fact on the case file.

9048.text: “block transfer order” has the meaning in Rule 54.228;

9049.text: “the Companies Law” means the Companies Law 2009;

9050.text: “the Court Law” means the DIFC Courts Law 2004;

9051.text: “insolvency proceedings” means any proceedings under the Law, the Regulations or this Part of these Rules ;

9053.text: “office-holder” means in relation to insolvency proceedings any person who by virtue of any provision of the Law or the Regulations holds an office in relation to those proceedings;

9054.text: “outgoing office-holder” has the meaning in Rule 54.227;

9055.text: “the petitioner” or “the petitioning creditor” includes, in winding up, any person who has been substituted as such, or has been given carriage of the petition;

9056.text: “the Regulations” means the Insolvency Regulations 2009 (and any reference to a numbered regulation is to that regulation within the Regulations);

9057.text: “replacement office-holder” has the meaning in Rule 54.227; and

9058.text: “substantive application” is that part of the application in Rule 54.230(1) and 54.230(2)

9060.text: For the purposes of this Part a “Company” includes:

9061.text: 54.2.1 a Recognised Company in respect of which a winding-up order is sought or which is being wound up by the Court pursuant to Part 6 of the Law save as otherwise provided or the context otherwise requires; and

9062.text: 54.2.2 a Foreign Company where and insofar as the context requires.

9064.text: Other terms which have a defined meaning in the Law, the Companies Law, the Regulations and these Rules have the same meaning in this Part.

9067.text: The provisions of Rules 2.11 to 2.15 (Time) apply, as regards computation of time, to anything required or authorised to be done in this Part.

9069.text: The provisions of Rule 4.2(1) (The Court’s general powers of management) apply so as to enable the Court to extend or shorten the time for compliance with anything required or authorised to be done by this Part.

9072.text: Save where these Rules provide otherwise, anything to be done under or by virtue of the Law, the Regulations or this Part of the Rules by, to or before the Court may be done by, to or before a Judge or a Registrar.

9076.text: This Section applies to any application made to the Court under the Law or the Regulations except (save as otherwise provided):

9077.text: (1) a petition for a winding-up order under Article 50 of the Law (see Section III);

9078.text: (2) an application for the appointment of a provisional liquidator (see Section IV); and

9079.text: (3) an application for a cell receivership order (see Section V).

9082.text: Each application shall be in writing and shall state:

9083.text: (1) that the application is made under the Law or the Regulations (as the case may be);

9085.text: (3) the name of the Company which is the subject of the insolvency proceedings to which the application relates;

9086.text: (4) where the Court has previously allocated a reference number to the insolvency proceedings within which the application is made, that number;

9087.text: (5) the nature of the remedy or order applied for or the directions sought from the Court ;

9088.text: (6) the names and addresses of the persons (if any) on whom it is intended to serve the application or that no person is intended to be served;

9089.text: (7) where the Law, the Regulations or these Rules require that notice of the application is to be given to specified persons, the names and addresses of all those persons (so far as known to the applicant); and

9093.text: On receipt of an application notice containing a request for a hearing the Court will notify the applicant of the time and date for the hearing of the application.

9095.text: Unless the Court otherwise directs, the applicant shall serve a sealed copy of the application, endorsed with the time and date for the hearing, on the respondent named in the application (or on each respondent if more than one).

9097.text: The Court may give any of the following directions:

9098.text: (1) that the application be served upon persons other than those specified by the relevant provision of the Law, the Regulations or these Rules ;

9099.text: (2) that the giving of notice to any person may be dispensed with; and

9100.text: (3) that notice be given in some way other than that specified in Rule 54.10.

9102.text: An application must be served at least fourteen (14) calendar days before the date fixed for its hearing unless:

9103.text: (1) the provision of the Law or the Regulations under which the application is made makes different provision; or

9104.text: (2) the case is one of urgency to which Rule 54.13 applies.

9106.text: Where the case is one of urgency, the Court may (without prejudice to its general power to extend or abridge time limits):

9107.text: (1) hear the application immediately, either with or without notice to, or the attendance of, other parties; or

9108.text: (2) authorise a shorter period of service than that provided for by Rule 54.12;

9109.text: and any such application may be heard on terms providing for the filing or service of documents, or the carrying out of other formalities, as the Court thinks fit.

9112.text: Where the relevant provisions of the Law or the Regulations do not require service of the application on, or notice of it to be given to, any person:

9113.text: (1) the Court may hear the application as soon as reasonably practicable without fixing a time and date for the hearing as required by Rule 54.9; or

9114.text: (2) it may fix a time and date for the hearing in which case Rules 54.9 to 54.13 shall apply to the extent that they are relevant;

9115.text: but nothing in those provisions is to be taken as prohibiting the applicant from giving such notice if the applicant wishes to do so.

9118.text: Subject to Rules 54.20 to 54.21, where evidence is required by the Law or the Regulations as to any matter, such evidence may be provided in the form of a witness statement unless:

9119.text: 54.15.1 in any specific case, the Act, a Regulation or a Rule makes different provision; or

9122.text: Witness statements and affidavits must comply with the requirements of Part 29 (Evidence), save that photocopy documents may be used unless the Court orders otherwise.

9124.text: Where in insolvency proceedings a witness statement is made by an office-holder, the witness statement must state:

9125.text: (1) the capacity in which that office-holder is acting; and (2) the address at which that office-holder works.

9127.text: The Court may, on the application of any party to the matter in question order the attendance for crossexamination of the person making the witness statement.

9129.text: Where, after such an order has been made, the person in question does not attend, that person’s witness statement must not be used in evidence without the permission of the Court .

9132.text: Unless the application involves other parties or the Court otherwise orders, a report may be filed in Court instead of a witness statement by:

9135.text: In any case where a report is filed instead of a witness statement, the report shall be treated for the purposes of Rule 54.22 and any hearing before the Court as if it were a witness statement.

9136.text: Filing And Service Of Witness Statements

9138.text: Unless the provision of the Law or the Regulations under which the application is made provides otherwise, or the Court otherwise allows:

9139.text: (1) if the applicant intends to rely at the first hearing on evidence in a witness statement, he shall file the witness statement with the Court and serve a copy on the respondent (or on each respondent if more than one);

9140.text: (2) where a respondent to an application intends to oppose it and to rely for that purpose on evidence in a witness statement, he shall file the witness statement with the Court and serve a copy on the applicant (or on each applicant if more than one) and on every other respondent; and

9141.text: (3) the timetable for the filing and service of witness statements shall be as set out in Rules 23.40 to 23.48.

9144.text: Unless the Court otherwise directs, the application will be heard in public in accordance with Article 13 of the Court Law .

9146.text: The jurisdiction of the Court to hear and determine an application may be exercised by the Registrar (to whom any application should be made in the first instance) unless:

9147.text:
(1) a direction to the contrary has been given; or   
(2) it is not within the Registrar’s power to make the order required (whether pursuant to Part 3 (Organisation of the Court) or otherwise).

9149.text: Nothing in Rule 54.24 precludes an application being made directly to the Judge in a proper case.

9151.text: Where an application is made to the Registrar , the Registrar may refer to the Judge any matter which the Registrar thinks should properly be decided by the Judge , and the Judge may either dispose of the matter or refer it back to the Registrar with such directions as that Judge thinks fit.

9154.text: The Court may adjourn the hearing of an application on such terms as it thinks fit.

9156.text: The Court may at any time give such directions as it thinks fit as to:

9157.text: (1) service or notice of the application on or to any person; (2) whether particulars of claim and defence are to be delivered and generally as to the procedure on

9158.text: the application, including whether a hearing is necessary; and

9159.text: (3) the matters to be dealt with in evidence.

9161.text: The Court may give directions as to the manner in which any evidence is to be adduced at a resumed hearing and in particular as to:

9162.text: (1) the taking of evidence wholly or partly by witness statement or orally; (2) the cross-examination of the maker of a witness statement; and (3) any report to be made by an office-holder.

9165.text: In any insolvency proceedings, orders of the Court may be enforced in the same manner as a judgment to the same effect in accordance with Part 45 (General Rules About Enforcement of Judgments and Orders).

9166.text: III PETITION FOR A WINDING-UP ORDER

9169.text: The Rules in this Section apply in relation to a winding up by the Court under Part 4, Chapter 5, or Part 6 of the Law.

9171.text: For the purposes of this Section, the term “a Company” includes a Recognised Company (with such modifications as may be necessary).

9172.text: Injunction To Restrain Presentation Or Advertisement Of Petition

9174.text: An application may at any time be made by a Company for an injunction to restrain a person from:

9175.text: (1) presenting a petition for the winding up of a Company; or (2) advertising a petition for the winding up of a Company.

9177.text: Such application should be made in accordance with Section II of this Part.

9180.text: The petition, verified by a statement of truth in accordance with Part 22 (Statements of Truth) and Rules 54.40 to 54.45, shall be filed in Court.

9182.text: There must be annexed to the petition (and each copy thereof) a copy of any written demand which has been served on the Company by the petitioner pursuant to Article 51.1(a) of the Law and Regulation 5.1.

9184.text: There shall be delivered with the petition:

9185.text: (1) if the petitioner is a person other than the Company, one copy for service on the Company;

9186.text: (2) if any office-holder has been appointed in respect of the Company or the assets of the Company (or any of them), one copy for service on each such office-holder; and

9187.text: (3) if the Company is an Authorised Person and the petitioner is not the DFSA , one copy for service on the DFSA for notification purposes.

9189.text: Each of the copies delivered shall have applied to it the seal of the Court , and shall be issued to the petitioner.

9191.text: The Court shall fix a date and time for the hearing of the petition, and this shall be endorsed on any copy of the petition issued to the petitioner under Rule 54.38.

9194.text: The petition shall be verified by a statement of truth in such form as the Court may specify.

9196.text: If the petition is in respect of debts due to different creditors, the debts to each creditor must be separately verified.

9198.text: A statement of truth which is not contained in or endorsed upon the petition which it verifies must be sufficient to identify the petition and must specify:

9199.text: (1) the Court reference number of the petition;

9200.text: (2) the name and registered number of the Company; and

9203.text: The statement of truth must be made:

9204.text: (1) by the petitioner (or if there are two or more petitioners, any one of them); (2) by some person such as a director, company secretary or similar company officer, or a legal representative, who has been concerned in the matters giving rise to the presentation of the petition; or (3) by some responsible person who is duly authorised by the petitioner to make the statement of truth and has the requisite knowledge of those matters.

9206.text: Where the person making the statement of truth is not the petitioner himself, or one of the petitioners, he must in the statement of truth identify himself and state:

9207.text: (1) the capacity in which, and the authority by which, he makes it; and

9208.text: (2) the means of his knowledge of the matters verified in the statement of truth.

9210.text: A statement of truth verifying more than one petition shall include in its title the names of the Companies to which it relates and shall set out, in respect of each Company, the statements relied on by the petitioner; and a clear and legible photocopy of the statement of truth must be filed with each petition which it verifies.

9211.text: Consent Of The Insolvency Practitioner Proposed For Appointment As Liquidator

9213.text: The petition shall contain the name and address of the insolvency practitioner whom the petitioner proposes should be appointed as liquidator or provisional liquidator of the Company in the event that a winding-up order is made by the Court and a statement that the person so nominated is (to the best of the petitioner’s belief) a registered insolvency practitioner for the purposes of Article 89 of the Law and has consented to such appointment. There shall be attached to the statement of truth a copy of the written consent to such appointment signed by the insolvency practitioner so nominated.

9216.text: Rules 54.48 to 54.52 apply as regards service of the petition on the Company (where the petitioner is other than the Company itself); and references to the petition are to a copy of the petition bearing the seal of the Court .

9218.text: Subject as follows, the petition shall be served at the Company’s registered office.

9220.text: Service of the petition at the registered office may be effected in any of the following ways:

9221.text: (1) it may be handed to a person who there and then acknowledges himself to be, or the best of the server’s knowledge, information and belief is, a director or other officer, or employee, of the Company;

9222.text: (2) it may be handed to a person who there and then acknowledges himself to be authorised to accept service of documents on the Company’s behalf; or

9223.text: (3) in the absence of any such person as is mentioned in paragraphs 54.49.1 or 54.49.2, it may be deposited at or about the registered office in such a way that it is likely to come to the notice of a person attending at the office.

9225.text: If for any reason service at the registered office is not practicable, or the Company has no registered office, the petition may be served on the Company by leaving at the Company’s last known principal place of business in such a way that it is likely to come to the attention of a person attending there, or by delivering it to the secretary or some director, manager or principal officer of the Company, wherever that person may be found.

9227.text: If for any reason it is impracticable to effect service as provided by Rules 54.49 or 54.50, the petition may be served in such other manner as the Court may by order approve or direct.

9229.text: Application for an order of the Court under Rule 54.51 may be made by an application without notice to any other party, supported by a witness statement, stating what steps have been taken to comply with Rules 54.49 and 54.50 and the reasons why it is impracticable to effect service as there provided.

9232.text: Service of the petition must be proved by a certificate of service.

9234.text: The certificate of service must be sufficient to identify the petition served and must specify:

9235.text: (1) the name and registered office of the Company;

9236.text: (2) the address of the registered office of the Company;

9239.text: Where substituted service has been ordered under Rule 54.51, the certificate of service must have attached to it a sealed copy of the order.

9241.text: The certificate of service must be filed in Court as soon as reasonably practicable after service, and in any event not less than five (5) business days before the hearing of the petition.

9242.text: Other Persons To Receive Copies Of Petition Or Entitled To Copy Of Petition

9244.text: If to the petitioner’s knowledge any office-holder has been appointed in respect of the Company or the assets of the Company (or any of them), a copy of the petition shall be sent by him to such office-holder.

9246.text: If to the petitioner’s knowledge the Company is an Authorised Person and the petitioner is not the DFSA itself, a copy of the petition shall be sent by the petitioner to the DFSA .

9248.text: A copy of the petition which is required to be sent pursuant to Rules 54.57 or 54.58 shall be despatched on the next business day after the day on which the petition is served on the Company.

9250.text: Subject to any order of the Court to the contrary every director, contributory and creditor of the Company is entitled to be furnished by the legal representative of the petitioner (or by the petitioner himself, if acting in person) with a copy of the petition within two (2) business days after requiring it on payment of reasonable copying charges.

9253.text: The petitioner shall cause the petition to be advertised, in accordance with Regulation 5.3, in the English

9254.text: language newspaper(s) and in the Arabic language newspaper(s) as the Court may by Practice Direction designate.

9256.text: Unless the Court otherwise directs, the notice must be made to appear:

9257.text: (1) if the petitioner is the Company itself, not less than seven (7) business days before the day appointed for the hearing; or

9258.text: (2) otherwise, not less than seven (7) business days after service of the petition on the Company, nor less than seven (7) business days before the day so appointed.

9260.text: In addition to the standard contents (as set out in Section VII), the notice must state:

9261.text: (1) that a petition has been presented for the winding up of the Company;

9262.text: (2) the name and address of the petitioner;

9263.text: (3) the date on which the petition was presented;

9264.text: (4) the date and time fixed for the hearing of the petition;

9265.text: (5) the name and address of the petitioner’s legal representative (if any);

9266.text: (6) the name and address of the insolvency practitioner whom the petitioner proposes for appointment as liquidator; and

9267.text: (7) that any person intending to appear at the hearing (whether to support or oppose the petition or the appointment of the insolvency practitioner proposed for appointment as liquidator) must give notice of that intention in accordance with Rules 54.70 to 54.73.

9269.text: Non-compliance with Rules 54.61 to 54.63, is a ground on which the Court may, if it thinks fit, dismiss the petition.

9272.text: The petitioner or his legal representative shall, at least five (5) business days before the hearing of the petition, file in Court a certificate of compliance with the Rules , in such form as the Court may specify, relating to service and advertisement.

9277.text: A copy or, where this is not reasonably practicable, a description of the form and content of any notice given shall be filed in Court with the certificate.

9279.text: Non-compliance with Rules 54.65 to 54.67 is a ground on which the Court may, if it thinks fit, dismiss the petition.

9282.text: If at least five (5) business days before the hearing the petitioner, on an application without notice to any other party in such form as the Court may specify, satisfies the Court that:

9284.text: the Court may order that the petitioner has permission to withdraw the petition on such terms as to costs as the parties may agree or the Court may think fit.

9287.text: Every person who intends to appear on the hearing of the petition shall give to the petitioner notice of his intention in accordance with Rules 54.71 to 54.73 in such form as the Court may specify.

9290.text: (1) the name and address of the person giving it, and any telephone number and reference which may

9291.text: be required for communication with him or with any other person (to be also specified in the notice) authorised to speak or act on his behalf;

9292.text: (2) whether his intention is to support or oppose the petition;

9293.text: (3) whether or not he objects (in the event that a winding-up order is made by the Court) to the appointment as liquidator or provisional liquidator of the insolvency practitioner proposed for such appointment by the petitioner;

9294.text: (4) whether, if he does so object, he proposes that (in the event that a winding-up order is made by the Court) some other insolvency practitioner be appointed liquidator or provisional liquidator; and

9295.text: (5) the amount and nature of his debt (if any).

9297.text: The notice shall be sent to the petitioner at the address shown for him in the Court records or in the advertisement of the petition required by Regulation 5.3, or it may be sent to his legal representative.

9299.text: The notice shall be sent so as to reach the addressee not later than 4 p.m. on the business day before that which is appointed for the hearing (or, where the hearing has been adjourned, for the adjourned hearing).

9301.text: A person who fails to comply with Rules 54.70 to 54.73 may appear on the hearing of the petition only with the permission of the Court .

9304.text: The petitioner shall prepare for the Court a list of the persons (if any) who have given notice under Rule 54.70 in such form as the Court may specify, specifying their names and addresses and (if known to him) their respective legal representatives.

9306.text: Against the name of each creditor in the list it shall be stated whether his intention is to support the petition, or to oppose it and whether he objects (in the event that a winding-up order is made by the Court ) to the appointment as liquidator or provisional liquidator of the insolvency practitioner proposed for such appointment by the petitioner.

9308.text: On the date appointed for the hearing of the petition, a copy of the list shall be lodged at Court or handed to the Court before the commencement of the hearing.

9310.text: If any permission is given under Rule 54.74, the petitioner shall add to the list the same particulars in respect of each person to whom permission has been given.

9313.text: If the Company intends to oppose the petition, it shall file in Court a witness statement in opposition not less than five (5) business days before the date fixed for the hearing.

9315.text: A copy of the witness statement shall be sent by the Company to the petitioner forthwith after filing.

9318.text: If the Court adjourns the hearing of the petition, unless the Court otherwise directs, the petitioner must forthwith send:

9319.text: (1) to the Company; and (2) where any creditor has given notice under Rule 54.70 but was not present at the hearing, to that creditor; notice of the order adjourning the petition, stating the date and time of the adjourned hearing.

9322.text: Where a person petitions and is subsequently found not entitled to do so, or where the petitioner:

9323.text: (1) fails to advertise his petition within the time prescribed by the Regulations or this Part of the Rules or such extended time as the Court may allow;

9324.text: (2) consents to withdraw his petition, or to allow it to be dismissed, consents to an adjournment, or fails to appear in support of his petition when it is called on in Court on the day originally fixed for the hearing, or on a day to which it is adjourned; or

9325.text: (3) appears, but does not apply for an order in the terms of the prayer of his petition;

9326.text: the Court may, on such terms as it thinks just, substitute as petitioner any person who in its opinion would have a right to present a petition, and who is desirous of prosecuting it.

9328.text: An order of the Court under Rule 54.82 may, where a petitioner fails to advertise his petition within the

9329.text: time prescribed by the Regulations or this Part of the Rules , or consents to withdraw his petition, be made at any time.

9332.text: Rules 54.85 to 54.91 apply when a winding-up order has been made by the Court .

9334.text: A winding-up order shall be in such form as the Court may specify, subject to any amendments or further provisions as the Court considers appropriate.

9336.text: The petitioner shall, not later than the business day following that on which the order is made, lodge with the Court a draft of the form in which he proposes the order be drawn up; and the petitioner and every other person who has appeared on the hearing of the petition shall leave at the Court all the documents required for enabling the order to be completed as soon as reasonably practicable.

9338.text: It is not necessary for the Court to appoint a date and time for any person to attend to settle the order, unless in any particular case the special circumstances make an appointment necessary.

9340.text: A winding-up order must name a person as the liquidator or provisional liquidator in respect of the conduct of the winding-up in accordance with Article 58(1) of the Law and Regulation 5.4(2).

9342.text: Three copies of the winding-up order, sealed with the seal of the Court , shall be sent as soon as reasonably practicable by the Court to the liquidator or provisional liquidator (as the case may be) in respect of the conduct of the winding-up.

9344.text: The liquidator or provisional liquidator shall cause:

9345.text: (1) a sealed copy of the order to be served on the Company by prepaid letter addressed to it at its registered office (if any) or, if there is no registered office, at its principal or last known principal place of business; and

9346.text: (2) a sealed copy of the order to be sent to the Registrar of Companies.

9348.text: Upon the making of a winding-up order, the Court shall forthwith in accordance with Regulation 5.4(1):

9349.text: (1) give notice of the fact to the Company, the petitioner, the DFSA (in the case of an Authorised Person) and any other person represented at the hearing of the petition; and

9350.text: (2) cause notice of the fact that the order has been made to be advertised, in accordance with Regulation 5.3, in the English language newspaper(s) and in the Arabic language newspaper(s) as the Court may by Practice Direction designate.

9353.text: Unless the Court otherwise directs, when a petition is dismissed, the petitioner shall forthwith give notice of the dismissal. Such notice shall be advertised in the same manner as the petition was advertised under Rules 54.61 to 54.64 or otherwise as the Court may direct.

9355.text: The notice advertised under Rule 54.92 must state:

9358.text: Where the petitioner is not the Company itself and the petitioner has not complied with Rules 54.92 and 54.93 within 21 days of the date of the hearing at which the petition was dismissed, the Company may advertise notice of the dismissal itself. Where the Company advertises notice of the dismissal itself, then unless the Court orders otherwise:

9359.text:
(1) the petitioner shall pay to the Company the costs reasonably incurred by the Company in advertising such notice; and   
(2) the amount of the costs so payable, unless agreed between the Company and the petitioner, must be decided by detailed assessment and the Company may commence detailed assessment proceedings in accordance with Part 40 (Procedure for Detailed Assessment of Costs).

9361.text: Rules 54.92 to 54.94 above do not apply in the case where a petition is withdrawn pursuant to Rule 54.69 above.

9364.text: The Court may at any time after an order for winding up, on the application of the provisional liquidator, the liquidator or the DFSA or any creditor or contributory, and on proof to the satisfaction of the Court that all proceedings in the winding up ought to be stayed make an order staying the proceedings, either altogether or for a limited time, on such terms and conditions as the Court thinks fit.

9366.text: The Court may, before making an order, give notice of the application to the DFSA , who may furnish the Court with a report with respect to any facts or matters which are in its opinion relevant to the application.

9368.text: A copy of every order made staying a winding up shall forthwith be forwarded by the Company to the Registrar of Companies.

9372.text: An application to the Court for the appointment of a provisional liquidator under Article 59 of the Law may be made by:

9375.text: The application must be supported by a witness statement stating:

9376.text: (1) the grounds on which it is proposed that a provisional liquidator should be appointed;

9377.text: (2) that the person proposed to be appointed has consented to act and, to the best of the applicant’s belief, is a registered insolvency practitioner for the purposes of Article 89 of the Law;

9379.text: (a) there has been proposed or is in force for the Company a voluntary arrangement under Part 2 of the Law;

9380.text: (b) an administrative receiver is acting in relation to the Company;

9381.text: (c) a liquidator has been appointed for the Company’s voluntary winding-up; or

9382.text: (d) a cell receivership order under Regulation 8.2 has been made in respect of the Company; and

9383.text: (4) the applicant’s estimate of the value of the assets in respect of which the provisional liquidator is to be appointed, and the facts and bases upon which such estimate is made.

9385.text: The Court may on the application, if satisfied that sufficient grounds are shown for the appointment, make it on such terms as it thinks fit.

9388.text: Where a provisional liquidator has been appointed, the Court shall as soon as reasonably practicable give notice of the fact to the provisional liquidator.

9390.text: Unless the Court otherwise directs, on receipt of the notice of appointment, as soon as reasonably practicable the provisional liquidator shall give notice of that appointment by advertisement once in the English language newspaper(s) and in the Arabic language newspaper(s) as the Court may by Practice Direction designate.

9392.text: In addition to the standard contents (as set out in Section VII), the notice under Rule 54.103 (to be in such form as the Court may specify) must state:

9393.text: (1) that a provisional liquidator has been appointed; and

9395.text: Order Of Appointment Of Provisional Liquidator

9397.text: The order appointing the provisional liquidator shall specify the functions to be carried out by him in relation to the Company’s affairs.

9399.text: The Court shall as soon as reasonably practicable after the order is made, send sealed copies of the orders as follows:

9400.text: (1) three copies to the person appointed as provisional liquidator; and

9401.text: (2) if there is an administrative receiver or cell receiver acting in relation to the Company, one copy to him.

9403.text: Of the three copies of the order sent to the provisional liquidator under Rule 54.106(1):

9404.text: (1) one copy shall be sent by him to the Company or, if a liquidator has been appointed for the Company’s voluntary winding-up, to him; and

9405.text: (2) one shall be sent to the Registrar of Companies.

9408.text: The cost of providing the security required under the Law upon the appointment of a provisional liquidator shall be paid in the first instance by the provisional liquidator; but:

9409.text: (1) if a winding-up order is not made, the person so appointed is entitled to be reimbursed out of the property of the Company, and the Court may make an order accordingly; and

9410.text: (2) if a winding-up order is made, he is entitled to be reimbursed as an expense of the liquidation in the prescribed order of priority.

9412.text: If the provisional liquidator fails to give or keep up his security, the Court may remove him and make such order as it thinks fit as to costs.

9414.text: If an order is made under Rule 54.109 removing the provisional liquidator, or discharging an order appointing him, the Court shall give directions as to whether any, and if so what, steps should be taken for the appointment of another person in his place.

9417.text: The remuneration of the provisional liquidator shall be fixed by the Court from time to time on his application.

9419.text: In fixing his remuneration, the Court shall take into account:

9420.text: (1) the time properly given by him (as provisional liquidator) and his staff in attending to the Company’s affairs;

9421.text: (2) the complexity (or otherwise) of the case;

9422.text: (3) any respects in which, in connection with the Company’s affairs, there falls on the provisional liquidator any responsibility of an exceptional kind or degree;

9423.text: (4) the effectiveness with which the provisional liquidator appears to be carrying out, or to have carried out, his duties; and

9424.text: (5) the value and nature of the property with which he has had to deal.

9426.text: Without prejudice to any order the Court may make as to costs, the provisional liquidator’s remuneration shall be paid to him, and the amount of any expenses incurred by him reimbursed:

9427.text: (1) if a winding-up order is not made, out of the property of the Company; and

9428.text: (2) if a winding-up order is made, as an expense of the liquidation, in the prescribed order of priority.

9430.text: Unless the Court otherwise directs, in a case falling within Rule 54.113(1) above the provisional liquidator may retain out of the company’s property such sums or property as are or may be required for meeting his remuneration and expenses.

9433.text: The appointment of the provisional liquidator shall be terminated without further order on the appointment of a liquidator and may be terminated by the Court on his application or on that of any of the persons specified in Rule 54.99.

9435.text: If the provisional liquidator’s appointment terminates, whether in consequence of the dismissal of the winding-up petition or otherwise, the Court may give such directions as it thinks fit with respect to the accounts of his administration or any other matters which it thinks appropriate.

9437.text: Notice of termination of the appointment of a provisional liquidator must be given by the provisional liquidator, unless the termination is on the making of a winding-up order or the Court otherwise directs. Such notice must as soon as reasonably practicable be:

9440.text: In addition to the standard contents (as set out in Section VII), a notice under Rule 54.117 must state:

9442.text: V APPLICATION FOR A CELL RECEIVERSHIP ORDER

9445.text: This Section applies in relation to the making of a cell receivership order under Regulation 8.2.

9446.text: Application For A Cell Receivership Order

9448.text: An application for a cell receivership application shall be in such form as the Court may specify.

9450.text: If made by the Company or by its directors, an application for a cell receivership order shall state the name of the Company and its address for service, which (in the absence of special reasons to the contrary) is that of the Company’s registered office.

9452.text: If the application is made by the directors, it shall state that it is made under Regulation 8.3.1(b); but from and after making it is to be treated for all purposes as the application of the Company.

9454.text: If made by any other person (including the DFSA ), an application for a cell receivership order shall state the name and address for service of the applicant.

9456.text: The application shall contain a statement of the applicant’s belief that the cellular assets attributable to a particular cell of the Company are or are likely to be insufficient to discharge the claims of creditors in respect of that cell.
